title: TPM_RC_SIZE intercepted as ResponseBufferTooSmall
description: In wolftpm_tss/connection.rs, do_transact explicitly checks for TPM_RC_SIZE (0x0095) before the generic Error::check path and maps it to Error::ResponseBufferTooSmall. This gives callers a typed, recoverable signal: they can retry the operation with a larger buffer. If TPM_RC_SIZE fell through to the generic Transport{code} path, callers would have to inspect the raw numeric code to distinguish 'my buffer was too small' from 'the TPM has a problem'. The shim also returns TPM_RC_SIZE when the caller-supplied rsp buffer is too small, so the signal propagates correctly end-to-end from C shim through Rust to the tpm2-rs Connection trait consumer. Alternative rejected: having the shim return a different sentinel value was considered but would require coordinating a non-standard error code; reusing the standard TPM_RC_SIZE is cleaner.
